Elon Musk misled Twitter investors while trying to get out of acquisition, jury says
articles.description: At the time, Musk had tweeted that Twitter had too many bots, which is why he was trying to renege on his commitment to buy the company.

articles.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
articles.description: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

articles.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet

articles.title: Amid legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
articles.description: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

articles.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
articles.description: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

articles.title: Why scientists can’t get a laugh
articles.description: Humor is hard to pull off in front of a cold audience, full stop.

articles.title: A French Navy officer accidentally leaked the location of an aircraft carrier by logging his run on Strava
articles.description: A French naval officer went on a run around the deck of the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, inadvertently leaking the warship's location when he uploaded the workout to Strava.

articles.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more
articles.description: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin enters the space data center game
articles.description: "Project Sunrise" would include more than 50,000 satellites performing high-energy compute on orbit.

articles.title: US accuses Iran’s government of operating hacktivist group that hacked Stryker
articles.description: The U.S. Justice Department said an Iranian security ministry operates the fake activist persona known as Handala, which claimed responsibility for the destructive hack targeting medical tech giant Stryker.

articles.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
articles.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

articles.title: AI startups are eating the venture industry and the returns, so far, are good
articles.description: AI startups accounted for 41% of the $128 billion in venture dollars raised by companies on Carta last year — a record-high annual share.

articles.title: Cyberattack on vehicle breathalyzer company leaves drivers stranded across the US
articles.description: A cyberattack on a U.S. car breathalyzer company has left drivers across the United States reportedly stranded and unable to start their vehicles.

articles.title: Amazon working on new smartphone with Alexa at its core, report says
articles.description: The device is being developed by the company's Devices and Services division, and it would feature personalized features aimed at making it easier to use Amazon's suite of apps, including Amazon shopping, Prime Video, and Prime Music, according to Reuters.

articles.title: The best AI investment might be in energy tech
articles.description: Power has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in rolling out new AI data centers. That's creating an opening for investors.

articles.title: These AI notetaking devices can help you record and transcribe your meetings
articles.description: These physical notetakers transcribe audio and give users summaries and action items of meetings using AI. Some even offer live translation.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Design Conductor: An agent autonomously builds a 1.5 GHz Linux-capable RISC-V CPU
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08716
AI agents now design production-grade silicon — not just code.
This crosses a significant threshold: an autonomous system producing a CPU that boots Linux at 1.5 GHz represents real hardware engineering capability, not a toy demo. The "vibecoding" tag is telling — we're seeing AI move from software generation into physical system design. Watch for compression in chip design cycles and talent dynamics at fabless semiconductor shops.

### Attention Residuals
URL: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Attention-Residuals
Moonshot AI publishes architectural research that could improve transformer efficiency.
Chinese AI labs continue shipping meaningful architecture research publicly. Attention mechanism improvements compound across the entire inference stack — every percentage point matters when you're burning $100M+ annually on compute. This is the kind of foundational work that determines who has cost advantages in 18 months.

### We rewrote our Rust WASM parser in TypeScript and it got faster
URL: https://www.openui.com/blog/rust-wasm-parser
Performance assumptions about systems languages don't always survive contact with real workloads.
Counterintuitive result with practical implications: the WASM boundary overhead and JIT optimization gaps can overwhelm Rust's raw speed advantages. This matters for the "rewrite it in Rust" movement driving infrastructure decisions. Performance engineering requires measurement, not ideology.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### AI startups are eating the venture industry and the returns, so far, are good
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/20/ai-startups-are-eating-the-venture-industry-and-the-returns-so-far-are-good/
AI captured 41% of the $128B deployed on Carta in 2025.
The concentration is remarkable and the "so far" qualifier is doing heavy lifting. Vintage 2023-2024 AI investments are showing paper returns, but the exit environment remains untested. This is either the beginning of a generational wealth creation event or a concentration risk that will blow up spectacularly. The answer depends entirely on whether revenue follows the cap tables.

### Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin enters the space data center game
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/20/jeff-bezos-blue-origin-enters-the-space-data-center-game/
"Project Sunrise" plans 50,000+ satellites for orbital compute.
Blue Origin pivoting from launch services to space infrastructure-as-a-service signals where the real margin opportunity lies. The power constraint thesis for terrestrial AI compute creates genuine demand for orbital alternatives. This is a multi-decade capex play — watch for AWS integration announcements.

### Elon Musk misled Twitter investors while trying to get out of acquisition, jury says
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/20/elon-musk-misled-twitter-investors-while-trying-to-get-out-of-acquisition-jury-says/
Jury finds Musk liable — damages phase incoming.
This creates discovery risk across the entire Twitter acquisition saga. The bot tweet that moved markets is now officially fraudulent. Secondary effects on Tesla governance and xAI fundraising dynamics are worth monitoring. Corporate boards taking notes on founder liability exposure.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through fitness app
URL: https://www.lemonde.fr/en/international/article/2026/03/20/stravaleaks-france-s-aircraft-carrier-located-in-real-time-by-le-monde-through-fitness-app_6751640_4.html
Consumer fitness data compromises naval operations security.
This is a clean case study in aggregation risk — individually innocuous data becomes operationally significant at scale. Defense ministries globally will now audit wearables policies. The real story is what this implies for corporate executives, facility locations, and M&A due diligence. Strava data is now officially an intelligence source.

### Trivy Compromised a Second Time - Malicious v0.69.4 Release
URL: https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/trivy-compromised-a-second-time---malicious-v0-69-4-release
Popular security scanner supply chain compromised — again.
Trivy scans containers for vulnerabilities across thousands of CI/CD pipelines. A compromised security tool has recursive blast radius. Second incident suggests systemic process failures, not isolated breach. Enterprise customers should be asking hard questions about their scanning infrastructure provenance.

### Entso-E final report on Iberian 2025 blackout
URL: https://www.entsoe.eu/publications/blackout/28-april-2025-iberian-blackout/
Official post-mortem on major European grid failure now public.
Grid stability analysis from the regulatory body. Renewable integration, interconnection dynamics, and cascading failure mechanisms are detailed. Relevant for anyone modeling European energy infrastructure investments or AI data center siting decisions on the continent.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The through-line this morning is infrastructure fragility meeting exponential demand. AI is consuming 41% of venture dollars while simultaneously straining power grids badly enough that Bezos is planning orbital data centers. The Strava-aircraft carrier story and Trivy supply chain compromise both illustrate how interconnected systems create non-obvious attack surfaces. The real alpha is in understanding which bottlenecks — power, security, talent — become binding constraints first.
